description: AIE Status Guidance Rules for MLDebug

rules.name: AIE Core Enabled Status
rules.description: Checks if AIE core is enabled
rules.bad_message: Core ({col},{row}) is disabled - check if design loaded correctly
rules.guidance: Ensure the AIE core is properly enabled. If disabled, verify that the PDI was loaded correctly and check if there are any configuration issues.

rules.name: Core Reset State Check
rules.description: Checks if core is in reset state
rules.guidance: Core is in reset state when it should be running. Verify that the PDI was loaded correctly and all cores were properly initialized. Check if there was a runtime error that triggered a reset.

rules.name: Core Lock Stall Check
rules.description: Checks if core is in lock stall state
rules.good_message: Core ({col},{row}) is not in lock stall
rules.bad_message: Core ({col},{row}) is in lock stall state
rules.guidance: Core is stalled waiting for a lock acquire. This indicates a deadlock or missing lock release. Check lock acquire/release sequences across tiles. Use lock status registers to identify which lock is causing the stall.

rules.name: Core Error Halt Check
rules.description: Checks if core is in error halt state
rules.good_message: Core ({col},{row}) is not in error halt
rules.bad_message: Core ({col},{row}) is in error halt state
rules.guidance: Core has halted due to an error condition. Common causes: data memory access out of range, invalid instruction, ECC errors. Check event status registers for specific error details. Review PC value and recent memory accesses.

rules.name: Lock Overflow Check
rules.guidance: Lock overflow indicates excessive lock acquires without releases. Review lock acquire/release pairing in your kernel code. This often indicates a synchronization bug.

rules.name: Lock Underflow Check
rules.guidance: Lock underflow indicates excessive lock releases without acquires. Review lock acquire/release pairing in your kernel code. This often indicates a synchronization bug.

rules.name: Core Event Errors
rules.description: Checks for critical error events in ERRORS_EVENT_REG (and ERRORS_EVENT_REG2 for NPU3): FP overflow/underflow/invalid, FP divide by zero, data memory out of range
rules.good_message: No critical error events on core ({col},{row})
rules.bad_message: Critical error event(s) detected on core ({col},{row}) - Status: {value}
rules.guidance: Core has critical error events. These can cause hangs or data mismatches. Check for: floating-point exceptions (overflow, underflow, invalid operation, divide by zero), data memory address out of range (can lead to ERROR_HALT state).

rules.name: Microcontroller Firmware Status
rules.description: Checks if microcontroller firmware is in running state
rules.guidance: Microcontroller is not running. Check firmware load status and look for error states. Review HSA queue status if applicable.
